candidate_problems.text: Can one formulate a precise, regulator-independent semiclassical gravity/EFT framework in which constant shifts of the matter Lagrangian are explicitly shown to be non-observable while the only gravitationally meaningful quantity is the renormalized invariant combination Λ_eff? The key unresolved tension is that standard semiclassical gravity appears to let vacuum stress gravitate, so the conjecture needs a sharp derivation of where the naive identification fails without merely redefining the problem away.

What concrete symmetry, constraint, or dynamical mechanism can drive Λ_eff toward a near-zero fixed point without fine-tuning, while remaining consistent with known quantum corrections, phase transitions, and cosmological history? The conjecture relocates the problem from summing zero-point energies to explaining the small invariant itself, but it leaves open which structure actually enforces smallness and why it yields a small nonzero value rather than exactly zero or radiative instability.

How does the conjecture handle known contributions that seem physically real in gravity-coupled QFT—such as vacuum changes across phase transitions, Casimir-like stresses, trace anomalies, and EFT matching across thresholds—without reintroducing the same tuning problem in disguised form? This is an important consistency test because if vacuum-energy differences gravitate in some contexts, one must explain why large sector-by-sector contributions do not accumulate into Λ_eff.
